questions.question: When does `align-content` work?
questions.explanation: `align-content` only works when `flex-wrap: wrap` or `wrap-reverse` creates multiple lines.

questions.question: What does `align-content` align?
options: Individual flex items within a line
Lines of flex items within the container
questions.explanation: `align-content` distributes space between and around lines of items along the cross axis.

questions.question: What is the difference between `align-content` and `align-items`?
options: align-content aligns lines; align-items aligns items within lines
align-items aligns lines; align-content aligns items
questions.explanation: `align-content` distributes entire lines; `align-items` aligns individual items within their line.

questions.question: What is the default value of `align-content`?
questions.explanation: The default is `normal` which behaves like `stretch` — lines stretch to fill the container.

questions.question: Which value distributes lines with equal space between them but none at edges?
questions.explanation: `space-between` puts equal space between lines, with first/last lines at container edges.

questions.question: Which align-content value pushes the first and last lines to the container edges and distributes remaining space between them?
questions.explanation: space-between places the first line at the start and last at the end, with equal space between remaining lines.

questions.question: What does align-content: center do in a multi-line flex container?
options: Centers the entire group of lines in the container
questions.explanation: align-content: center centers all lines as a group within the cross axis of the container.

questions.question: What is the effect of align-content: flex-start on wrapped flex items?
options: Lines pack at the start of the cross axis
questions.explanation: flex-start packs all lines at the beginning of the cross axis with no extra space between them.

questions.question: What is the difference between space-around and space-evenly for align-content?
options: space-around gives half-size space at edges; space-evenly gives equal space everywhere
space-evenly has half-size space at edges
questions.explanation: With space-around, the first and last lines get half-size space from the edge. With space-evenly, all spacing is equal.

questions.question: Which scenario requires align-content instead of align-items?
options: A flex container with flex-wrap: wrap and multiple lines of items
questions.explanation: align-content is specifically for distributing space between multiple lines in a wrapped flex container.

questions.question: What does align-content: stretch do in a multi-line flex container?
options: Lines stretch to fill the cross axis evenly
Items stretch to fill their line
questions.explanation: stretch (the default) distributes remaining space equally among lines, causing them to stretch.

questions.question: What property must be set for align-content to have any visible effect?
questions.explanation: align-content only has a visible effect when flex-wrap creates multiple lines of items.

questions.question: With flex-direction: column and flex-wrap: wrap, what axis does align-content control?
questions.explanation: With column direction, the main axis is vertical and cross axis is horizontal. align-content controls distribution on the cross axis.

questions.question: Which align-content value creates equal spacing before, between, and after every line?
questions.explanation: space-evenly distributes equal space before the first line, between lines, and after the last line.

questions.question: What does align-content: flex-end do with wrapped items?
options: Packs all lines at the end of the cross axis
Moves items to the bottom of each line
questions.explanation: flex-end packs all lines at the end of the cross axis, leaving space at the beginning.

questions.question: Can align-content and align-items be used together?
options: Yes, they control different aspects of alignment
questions.explanation: align-content distributes lines within the container; align-items aligns items within each line. They work together.

questions.question: In a wrapped flex container with extra space on the cross axis, which property controls where the lines sit?
questions.explanation: align-content controls how multiple lines are distributed along the cross axis when there's extra space.

questions.question: What is a common use case for align-content?
options: Distributing rows of wrapped cards evenly in a container
questions.explanation: When cards or items wrap to multiple rows, align-content controls the vertical distribution of those rows.

questions.question: Which is NOT a valid align-content value?
questions.explanation: space-inside is not a valid CSS value. Valid values include flex-start, flex-end, center, space-between, space-around, space-evenly, and stretch.

questions.question: What happens to align-content when flex-wrap: nowrap is set?
options: It has no visible effect — only one line exists
questions.explanation: With nowrap (default), there is only one line, so align-content has nothing to distribute and no visible effect.
